topics.title: Creating Web Page Using HTML5: Introduction, First HTML5 Example, Headings, Linking, Images, Special Characters and Horizontal Rules, Lists, Tables, Forms, Internal Linking, Meta Elements, HTML5 Form Input Types, Input and Datalist Elements and Autocomplete Attribute, Page-Structure Elements
topics.content:
> **APJ ABDUL KALAM TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY (KTU) | 2024 SCHEME** > - **Course:** OECST832 - WEB PROGRAMMING > - **Module:** Module 1 > - **Topic:** Creating Web Page Using HTML5: Introduction, First HTML5 Example, Headings, Linking, Images, Special Characters and Horizontal Rules, Lists, Tables, Forms, Internal Linking, Meta Elements, HTML5 Form Input Types, Input and Datalist Elements and Autocomplete Attribute, Page-Structure Elements # SECTION 1: Core Technical Definition & Intuitive Overview ## 1.1 What is HTML5? **HTML5 (HyperText Markup Language, version 5)** is the fifth and current major version of the standard HTML used to structure and present content on the World Wide Web. It is governed by the **W3C (World Wide Web Consortium)** and the **WHATWG (Web Hypertext Application Technology Working Group)**. HTML5 is not merely a markup language — it is a **complete application-development platform** that combines HTML, CSS3, and JavaScript APIs to deliver rich, semantic, and interactive web experiences. > [!NOTE] > **KTU Syllabus Definition (2024 Scheme):** > *HTML5 is a markup language used for structuring and presenting content on the World Wide Web. It is the fifth and final major HTML version that is a World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) recommendation.* ### Formal Structural Anatomy of an HTML5 Document Every valid HTML5 document must declare a document type and follow a specific tree-based structure: ```html First HTML5 Page

Hello, KTU!

Welcome to Web Programming.

``` - `` → Declares the document as HTML5. It is **case-insensitive** and must be the **very first line**. - `` → The root element wrapping all page content. - `` → Container for machine-readable metadata (not displayed on the page). - `` → Contains the visible content of the webpage. > [!IMPORTANT] > **KTU High-Yield Point:** HTML5 is **NOT case-sensitive** for tag names (e.g., `

` and `

` are equivalent). However, KTU best-practice and W3C recommendation strongly prefer **lowercase tags** for consistency and XHTML compatibility. ### Conceptual Analogy: The Skeleton of a Building Imagine you are constructing a **building**: | HTML5 Element | Real-World Building Equivalent | |---------------|-------------------------------| | `` | The **building permit** declaring it follows the 2024 code | | `` | The **entire building structure** | | `` | The **foundation & utility room** (hidden but essential) | | `` | The **rooms, walls, and furniture** (what visitors see) | | `

` to `

` | The **building's name boards and floor signs** | | `

` | The **descriptive plaques** on each room | | `` (anchor) | The **doorways & elevators** connecting rooms | | `` | The **windows and picture frames** | | `` | The **filing cabinets** of an office | | `` | The **reception desk** collecting visitor information | > [!TIP] > **Intuition Check:** When a browser (like Chrome or Firefox) receives an HTML5 file, it parses it **top-to-bottom** like reading a book, building a **DOM (Document Object Model) tree** in memory. Every tag becomes a node; every attribute becomes a node property. --- ## 1.2 Your First HTML5 Example — Line-by-Line Explanation Let us dissect the **canonical "Hello, World"** of HTML5: ```html My First Page

Hello, World!

This is my first HTML5 page.

``` > [!NOTE] > **Why ``?** > UTF-8 is a **variable-width character encoding** capable of encoding all 1,112,064 valid code points in Unicode. It supports virtually every language, emoji, and special symbol — making it the **de facto standard** for the modern web. --- ## 1.3 Headings — The `

` to `

` Family HTML5 provides **six levels of headings**, ranked from most important (`

`) to least important (`

`). These are **block-level** semantic elements. ```html

Level 1 — Main Title

Level 2 — Section Heading

Level 3 — Subsection

Level 4 — Sub-subsection

Level 5 — Minor Heading
Level 6 — Smallest Heading
``` > [!IMPORTANT] > **KTU Exam Tip:** A single HTML5 document should contain **only ONE `

` tag** for SEO and accessibility (W3C accessibility guidelines). Using multiple `

` tags is valid HTML5 but discouraged. --- ## 1.4 Linking — The `` Anchor Element The `` (anchor) element creates a **hyperlink** — the very feature that makes the web a "web." It uses the `href` attribute to specify the destination. ```html Visit KTU Official Site Open in New Tab Email Admin Call KTU Download Report ``` ### Key Attributes of ``: | Attribute | Purpose | Example | |-----------|---------|---------| | `href` | URL or path of the target resource | `href="about.html"` | | `target` | Where to open the linked document | `target="_blank"` | | `rel` | Relationship between current and linked doc | `rel="noopener"` | | `download` | Prompts download (no navigation) | `download="file.pdf"` | | `title` | Tooltip text on hover | `title="Click here"` | > [!TIP] > **Intuition:** Think of an anchor tag as a **portal or teleporter**. The text between `` and `` is the **"sign"** you see; the `href` is the **destination coordinates**. --- ## 1.5 Images — The `` Element The `` element embeds an image into the page. It is a **void (self-closing) element** with no closing tag. ```html ``` ### Required and Recommended Attributes: | Attribute | Mandatory? | Purpose | |-----------|-----------|---------| | `src` | **Yes** | Specifies the path/URL of the image | | `alt` | **Yes (Accessibility)** | Alternative text for screen readers & broken images | | `width` | No | Display width in pixels (or `%`) | | `height` | No | Display height in pixels (or `%`) | | `loading` | No | `"lazy"` defers off-screen image loading | | `title` | No | Tooltip on hover | > [!WARNING] > **Always provide `alt` text!** It's mandatory for WCAG 2.1 accessibility compliance. If the image is purely decorative, use `alt=""` (empty). --- ## 1.6 Special Characters and Horizontal Rules ### Special Characters (HTML Entities) Some characters have special meaning in HTML (e.g., `<`, `>`, `&`) and cannot be typed directly. We use **HTML entities** — a sequence beginning with `&` and ending with `;`. | Character | Entity Name | Entity Number | Description | |-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------| | `<` | `<` | `<` | Less-than sign | | `>` | `>` | `>` | Greater-than sign | | `&` | `&` | `&` | Ampersand | | `"` | `"` | `"` | Double quotation mark | | `'` | `'` | `'` | Apostrophe (single quote) | | `©` | `©` | `©` | Copyright symbol | | `®` | `®` | `®` | Registered trademark | | non-breaking space | ` ` | ` ` | Invisible spacing | ```html

5 < 10 && 10 > 5

© 2024 KTU. All rights reserved.

``` ### Horizontal Rules — `
` The `
` element creates a **thematic break** — a horizontal line separating content. In HTML5, its meaning is **semantic**: it represents a transition between paragraphs or sections. ```html

Section 1 content...

Section 2 content...

``` > [!NOTE] > In HTML5, `
` is a **void element** (no closing tag). In older HTML4, it was a presentational tag. HTML5 redefined it as **semantic** — use CSS to style its appearance. # SECTION 2: Deep Theoretical Analysis & KTU High-Yield Reference ## 2.1 Lists — Three Flavors in HTML5 HTML5 provides three list types, each with distinct **semantic meaning** (not just visual appearance). ### 2.1.1 Ordered List `
    ` Used when the **order of items is significant** (e.g., instructions, rankings). ```html
    1. Step One: Open VS Code
    2. Step Two: Create index.html
    3. Step Three: Save the file
    ``` | Attribute | Values | Effect | |-----------|--------|--------| | `type` | `1`, `A`, `a`, `I`, `i` | Numbering style | | `start` | integer | Starting value | | `reversed` | boolean | Counts down | ### 2.1.2 Unordered List `
      ` Used when order is **not significant** (e.g., navigation menus, feature lists). ```html
      • HTML5
      • CSS3
      • JavaScript
      ``` ### 2.1.3 Description List `
      ` A list of **term-description pairs** (perfect for glossaries, FAQs, metadata). ```html
      HTML
      HyperText Markup Language — the standard markup language for web pages.
      CSS
      Cascading Style Sheets — used for styling HTML documents.
      ``` > [!IMPORTANT] > **KTU Exam Note:** `
      `, `
      `, and `
      ` are commonly tested. Remember: **D**escription **L**ist contains **D**escription **T**erms and **D**escription **D**etails. --- ## 2.2 Tables — The `

` Element HTML5 tables organize data into **rows and columns**. They are **not** to be used for layout (use CSS Grid/Flexbox for that). ```html
KTU B.Tech Result Summary
Roll No Name SGPA
KTU001 Arjun 9.2
KTU002 Meera 8.8
Class Average: 9.0
``` ### Structural Elements: | Element | Purpose | |---------|---------| | `` | The container element | | `` | Groups header rows | | `` | Groups data rows | | `` | Groups footer rows | | `` | A table row | | `` is the **column header bar**, `` is the **data area**, and `` is the **totals row** at the bottom. --- ## 2.3 Forms — The `` Element Forms are the **primary mechanism for user input** and interaction on the web. They collect data and submit it to a server for processing. ```html
Login Information
``` ### Key `` Attributes: | Attribute | Purpose | |-----------|---------| | `action` | URL where form data is submitted | | `method` | HTTP method: `GET` (default) or `POST` | | `enctype` | Encoding type (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, `multipart/form-data`, `text/plain`) | | `target` | Where to display the response (`_self`, `_blank`, `_parent`, `_top`) | | `autocomplete` | Browser auto-fills form (`on` or `off`) | | `novalidate` | Disables HTML5 form validation | ### GET vs POST — KTU Comparison Table: | Property | GET | POST | |----------|-----|------| | Data visibility | Appended to URL (visible in address bar) | Sent in HTTP request body (hidden) | | Length limit | **~2048 characters** (browser-dependent) | **No length limit** | | Security | Less secure (data in URL) | More secure (data in body) | | Bookmarkable | Yes (URL contains parameters) | No | | Caching | Can be cached | Not cached | | Idempotent | Yes (safe to repeat) | No (may cause side effects) | | Use case | Search queries, filters | Login forms, file uploads, payments | --- ## 2.4 Internal Linking (Bookmarks / Fragment Links) Internal links navigate within the **same page** using **fragment identifiers** — the `#` symbol followed by an `id`. ```html

HTML5 Forms

Forms collect user data...

Jump to Forms SectionJump to Tables ``` **Mechanism in 3 Steps:** 1. The destination element has a unique `id` attribute. 2. The link's `href` starts with `#` followed by that `id`. 3. The browser scrolls the destination element to the top of the viewport. > [!NOTE] > **Real-World Use:** Single-page documentation sites (like KTU syllabus PDFs converted to HTML) use fragment links extensively for the **Table of Contents**. --- ## 2.5 Meta Elements — The `` Tag The `` element provides **metadata about the HTML document**. It is a void element placed inside ``. ```html ``` ### Major Meta Variants: | Attribute | Purpose | Example | |-----------|---------|---------| | `charset` | Character encoding | `UTF-8` | | `name="viewport"` | Responsive design control | `width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0` | | `name="description"` | SEO summary (~155 chars) | `"KTU B.Tech course notes"` | | `name="keywords"` | SEO keywords (less important now) | `"HTML5, CSS, JS"` | | `name="author"` | Author name | `"KTU Faculty"` | | `http-equiv="refresh"` | Auto-reload or redirect | `content="5; url=https://ktu.edu.in"` | | `name="robots"` | Search engine indexing directives | `"index, follow"` or `"noindex"` | > [!IMPORTANT] > **KTU Exam Note:** The `` tag is **essential for mobile-responsive design**. Without it, mobile browsers render the page at desktop width and then scale it down, breaking layouts. --- ## 2.6 HTML5 Form Input Types — The Complete Catalogue HTML5 introduced **13 new input types** that enable native validation and specialized keyboards on mobile devices. ```html I agree Male Female ``` ### Comprehensive HTML5 Input Types Table: | Input Type | Purpose | Key Attributes | |------------|---------|----------------| | `text` | Single-line text | `maxlength`, `size`, `pattern` | | `password` | Masked text input | `minlength`, `required` | | `email` | Email address with validation | `multiple`, `required` | | `url` | URL with validation | `required` | | `tel` | Telephone number | `pattern`, `placeholder` | | `number` | Numeric input with spinners | `min`, `max`, `step` | | `range` | Slider control | `min`, `max`, `step`, `value` | | `date` | Date picker (YYYY-MM-DD) | `min`, `max` | | `time` | Time picker (HH:MM) | `min`, `max`, `step` | | `datetime-local` | Date + time, no timezone | `min`, `max` | | `month` | Month + year picker | `min`, `max` | | `week` | Week + year picker | `min`, `max` | | `color` | Color picker | `value` (hex) | | `file` | File upload | `accept`, `multiple` | | `search` | Search field (with clear X) | `placeholder` | | `hidden` | Invisible data carrier | `name`, `value` | | `checkbox` | Multi-select boxes | `checked`, `required` | | `radio` | Single-select from group | `checked`, `name` | | `submit` | Submit form button | `value` | | `reset` | Reset form fields | `value` | | `button` | Generic clickable button | `onclick` | | `image` | Image as submit button | `src`, `alt` | --- ## 2.7 The `` Element & Autocomplete Attribute ### 2.7.1 `` — Predefined Suggestion List The `` element provides a **list of predefined options** for an ``. Unlike ` ``` **Behavior:** When the user types, the browser shows a dropdown of matching `
` | Accessible title of the table (read by screen readers) | | `
` | A header cell (bold, centered by default) | | `` | A data cell | ### Key Attributes: | Attribute | Applied To | Purpose | |-----------|-----------|---------| | `colspan` | ``, `` | Spans across multiple **columns** | | `rowspan` | ``, `` | Spans across multiple **rows** | | `headers` | `` | Associates cell with `` id (accessibility) | | `scope` | `` | `row`, `col`, `rowgroup`, `colgroup` | > [!TIP] > **Intuition:** A table is a **spreadsheet grid**. `
`, ``, `` structure rows by purpose (header, body, footer). - `scope="col"` and `scope="row"` are **accessibility attributes** that announce whether a header cell applies to a column or a row. - `colspan="3"` makes the "Average SGPA" cell span **3 columns**. ### Step 5: Feedback Form with HTML5 Validation ```html

Send Feedback

Personal Information







5/10

Your Message



``` **Explanation of form features:** - `
` + `` group related form controls — improves accessibility. - The `` provides **suggestions** (B.Tech CSE, ECE, etc.) but the user can still type any value. - `` provides a **slider**; the `` element displays its current value. - `autocomplete="name"` and `autocomplete="email"` use **standard tokens** from the WHATWG spec — browsers like Chrome use them to auto-fill from saved profiles. - `required`, `minlength`, `min`, `max` enable **native HTML5 validation** without JavaScript. ### Step 6: Aside, Footer & Closing Tags ```html

Quick Links

  • KTU Official
  • Exam Portal

© 2024 Arjun Kumar. Last updated: November 15, 2024

Contact: arjun@ktu.ac.in
``` **Final explanation:** - `
` holds **tangentially related** content (sidebar). - `
` holds closing metadata; `
` is **semantically** for contact info (browsers may render it with italic style by default). - `` provides a **machine-readable date** while displaying human-friendly text. --- ## 3.2 Walkthrough 2: HTML5 Input Validation Logic Flowchart Although HTML5 validation is not algorithmic, its behavior follows a **clear decision tree**. Below is the analytical matrix for how browsers process a form submission. ### Browser Validation Sequence: | Step | Browser Action | User Experience | |------|---------------|-----------------| | 1 | User clicks `` | — | | 2 | Browser scans all form fields with `required` attribute | — | | 3 | For each empty `required` field, browser blocks submission and shows a tooltip: *"Please fill out this field"* | User sees red outline on empty field | | 4 | For `type="email"` fields, browser checks for `@` and domain pattern | Tooltip: *"Please include an '@' in the email address"* | | 5 | For `type="url"` fields, browser checks for protocol (http/https) | Tooltip: *"Please enter a URL"* | | 6 | For `type="number"`, browser checks `min`, `max`, `step` constraints | Tooltip: *"Value must be greater than or equal to X"* | | 7 | For `pattern="regex"`, browser tests value against the regex | Tooltip: *"Please match the requested format"* | | 8 | For `minlength`/`maxlength`, browser checks character count | Tooltip: *"Please lengthen this text to X characters or more"* | | 9 | If all checks pass, browser serializes the form and submits it to the `action` URL via the `method` | Browser navigates to the action URL | | 10 | If `novalidate` is set on `
`, browser **skips steps 2–8** and submits directly | — | > [!TIP] > **Intuition:** HTML5 validation is like a **gatekeeper at a building entrance**. The `required` attribute is the **ID check**; `pattern` is the **dress code**; `min`/`max` is the **age restriction**. The form is submitted only when every gate is passed. --- ## 3.3 Walkthrough 3: GET vs POST — Side-by-Side Construction ### Same form, two different methods: ```html ``` When submitted with `q="web programming"`, the browser navigates to: $$\text{URL} = \text{action} + \text{"?"} + \text{serialized-data}$$ $$= \texttt{/search?q=web+programming}$$ ```html
``` **HTTP Request generated by POST:** ``` POST /login HTTP/1.1 Host: ktu.edu.in Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded Content-Length: 35 username=arjun&password=secret123 ``` > [!WARNING] > **Security Pitfall:** Even with POST, passwords are sent as **plaintext** unless HTTPS is used. HTML5 cannot encrypt data — that is the job of **TLS/SSL**. Always use `https://` for sensitive forms. --- ## 3.4 Analytical Matrix: Datalist vs Select | Property | `` | |----------|-----------|------------------------| | User can type custom value | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | | User can submit empty | ❌ Required by default | ✅ Allowed (unless `required` is set) | | Display style | Always a dropdown | Input box with suggestion popup | | Accessibility | Fully supported | Supported but less mature | | Mobile UX | Native picker | Native keyboard + suggestions | | Best use case | Strict choice (country, gender) | Suggestions (city, course, tag) | > [!IMPORTANT] > **KTU 2024 Note:** When asked to "create a form with predefined options", students often confuse ``. # SECTION 4: Structural Diagrams & Schematics > All diagrams use **Mermaid syntax** with alphanumeric node IDs and plain-text labels (no markdown formatting inside labels) to avoid rendering errors. --- ## 4.1 The HTML5 Document Tree (DOM Structure) ```mermaid graph TD A[DOCTYPE html] --> B[html lang=en] B --> C[head] B --> D[body] C --> C1[meta charset=UTF-8] C --> C2[meta name=viewport] C --> C3[title] C --> C4[meta name=description] D --> E[header] D --> F[main] D --> G[footer] E --> E1[h1 Page Title] E --> E2[nav] E2 --> E3[ul] E3 --> E4[li - a href] F --> F1[article id=about] F --> F2[section id=skills] F --> F3[section id=contact] F1 --> F1A[figure] F1A --> F1B[img src] F1A --> F1C[figcaption] F2 --> F2A[ul] F3 --> F3A[form action method] F3A --> F3B[fieldset] F3B --> F3C[legend] F3B --> F3D[label for] F3B --> F3E[input type text] F3B --> F3F[input type email] F3B --> F3G[input type submit] G --> G1[p copyright] G --> G2[address] ``` --- ## 4.2 HTML5 Input Type Decision Tree ```mermaid graph TD A[Need to collect user input] --> B{What kind of data?} B -->|Free text| C[type=text] B -->|Sensitive| D[type=password] B -->|Email format| E[type=email] B -->|Web URL| F[type=url] B -->|Phone number| G[type=tel] B -->|Numeric with arrows| H[type=number] B -->|Numeric slider| I[type=range] B -->|Date picker| J[type=date] B -->|Time picker| K[type=time] B -->|Date and time| L[type=datetime-local] B -->|Month picker| M[type=month] B -->|Week picker| N[type=week] B -->|Color picker| O[type=color] B -->|File upload| P[type=file] B -->|Search box| Q[type=search] B -->|Boolean flag| R{True or False?} R -->|Yes/No| S[type=checkbox] R -->|One of N| T[type=radio] B -->|Trigger action| U{What kind?} U -->|Submit form| V[type=submit] U -->|Clear form| W[type=reset] U -->|Custom JS| X[type=button] B -->|Hidden value| Y[type=hidden] ``` --- ## 4.3 Form Submission Lifecycle (GET vs POST) ```mermaid flowchart TD A[User fills form] --> B[User clicks submit button] B --> C{Form has novalidate?} C -->|Yes| E[Skip all validation] C -->|No| D[Run HTML5 validation checks] D --> D1{All required fields filled?} D1 -->|No| D2[Show browser tooltip - block submit] D2 --> A D1 -->|Yes| D3{Type-specific validation passes?} D3 -->|No| D2 D3 -->|Yes| E E --> F{Method attribute value?} F -->|GET| G1[Serialize data as query string] G1 --> G2[Append to action URL with ?] G2 --> G3[Browser navigates to new URL] G3 --> H[Server receives parameters via URL] F -->|POST| I1[Serialize data as form body] I1 --> I2[Send in HTTP request body] I2 --> I3[Browser shows response from server] I3 --> J[Server processes hidden body data] ``` --- ## 4.4 Semantic Page-Structure Elements Layout ```mermaid graph TD A[html] --> B[head - hidden metadata] A --> C[body - visible content] C --> D[header - top banner] C --> E[main - primary content - only one] C --> F[aside - sidebar] C --> G[footer - bottom info] D --> D1[site logo and h1] D --> D2[nav - main menu] E --> E1[article - standalone content] E --> E2[section - thematic block 1] E --> E3[section - thematic block 2] E1 --> E1A[header of article] E1 --> E1B[figure and figcaption] E1 --> E1C[p paragraphs] E1 --> E1D[footer of article - metadata] E2 --> E2A[h2 heading] E2 --> E2B[p and lists] E3 --> E3A[h2 heading] E3 --> E3B[form] F --> F1[h3 widget title] F --> F2[related links list] G --> G1[copyright and p] G --> G2[address contact info] ``` --- ## 4.5 Datalist & Autocomplete Interaction Flow ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Browser participant Input as Input Field participant Datalist as Datalist Options participant User User->>Input: Types letter B Input->>Browser: Request matching options Browser->>Datalist: Filter options starting with B Datalist-->>Browser: B.Tech CSE, B.Tech ECE, B.Tech ME Browser-->>Input: Show suggestion dropdown User->>Input: Selects B.Tech CSE Input-->>User: Value filled - B.Tech CSE Note over User,Input: User can also type custom value
and submit it Browser->>Browser: On next visit - autocomplete=off can disable history ``` --- ## 4.6 Block-Level Functional Architecture: Complete Web Page Pipeline ```mermaid flowchart LR A[User opens browser] --> B[Browser requests URL from server] B --> C[Server returns HTML5 file] C --> D[Browser parses DOCTYPE] D --> E[Browser builds DOM tree] E --> F[Browser reads head metadata] F --> G[Browser fetches external resources] G --> G1[CSS files] G --> G2[JavaScript files] G --> G3[Images and media] E --> H[Browser renders body content] H --> H1[Header with nav] H --> H2[Main with article and section] H --> H3[Form for interaction] H --> H4[Aside for sidebar] H --> H5[Footer with address] H3 --> I[User submits form] I --> J{Method} J -->|GET| K[Data in URL] J -->|POST| L[Data in body] K --> M[Server response] L --> M M --> N[New page rendered] ``` # SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap --- ## 5.1 Part A Questions (2 × 3 = 6 Marks) ### Question 1 (3 Marks) `[KTU University Exam - May 2024]` **Q: List any six new input types introduced in HTML5 and state one attribute of each.** **Model Answer:** | # | Input Type | Purpose | Key Attribute | |---|-----------|---------|---------------| | 1 | `email` | Validates email format | `multiple` (allow multiple emails) | | 2 | `url` | Validates URL format | `placeholder` | | 3 | `number` | Numeric input with spinners | `min`, `max`, `step` | | 4 | `range` | Slider for numeric range | `min`, `max`, `value` | | 5 | `date` | Date picker (YYYY-MM-DD) | `min`, `max` | | 6 | `color` | Color picker | `value` (hex code) | | 7 | `tel` | Telephone number | `pattern` (regex) | | 8 | `file` | File upload | `accept`, `multiple` | | 9 | `time` | Time picker | `step` | | 10 | `search` | Search field | `placeholder` | (Any six correct entries with one attribute each — **Full 3 marks**) --- ### Question 2 (3 Marks) `[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]` **Q: Differentiate between `` | `` | |----------|-----------|--------------| | Tag pairing | Paired with `
` | | Cell merge | `colspan="n"` / `rowspan="n"` | | Form | `
` | | Internal link | `` | | Meta description | `` | | HTML5 input types | `email, url, tel, number, range, date, time, color, file` | | Datalist binding | `` paired with `` | | Autocomplete | `on`, `off`, or tokens like `name`, `email`, `current-password` | | Semantic sections | `header, nav, main, article, section, aside, footer` | # SECTION 3: Step-by-Step Construction & Analytical Matrix > This section demonstrates the **step-by-step construction** of progressively complex HTML5 documents. Since this is a markup topic (not a mathematical one), "derivations" are replaced by **structured walkthroughs of full working examples** — every line is annotated, every attribute is explained. --- ## 3.1 Walkthrough 1: A Complete Personal Portfolio Page (14-Mark Style) **Problem Statement (KTU-style):** *Create an HTML5 page titled "Student Portfolio" with a heading, your image, a list of skills, a table of academic records, a feedback form, and semantic page-structure elements.* ### Step 1: Document Skeleton & Metadata ```html Student Portfolio | KTU B.Tech ``` **Explanation of each line:** - **Line 1** declares the document follows the HTML5 standard. Without it, browsers enter "quirks mode" (legacy rendering). - **Line 2** opens the root element and sets the document's primary language to English (helps screen readers pronounce words correctly). - **Line 3** begins the metadata section. - **Line 4** specifies UTF-8 encoding so all Unicode characters render correctly. - **Line 5** is the **viewport meta tag** — essential for mobile responsiveness. It tells mobile browsers: "render the page at the device's native width, with 1:1 zoom." - **Line 6** provides an SEO description (≈155 characters recommended). - **Line 7** identifies the author. - **Line 8** sets the browser tab title. ### Step 2: Header & Navigation ```html

Arjun Kumar — B.Tech CSE

APJ Abdul Kalam Technological University

  • About
  • Skills
  • Academics
  • Contact
``` **Explanation:** - `
` groups the page's introductory content (logo, title, navigation). - The `
` element wraps the **primary navigation links**. Search engines and screen readers recognize this as the site menu. - All four links are **internal fragment links** using `#` — they will scroll within the same page. ### Step 3: Main Content with `
` and `
` ```html

About Me

I am a passionate web developer learning HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript.

Technical Skills

  • HTML5 & Semantic Markup
  • CSS3 & Flexbox/Grid
  • JavaScript (ES6+)
  • Python & Django
``` **Explanation:** - `
` is the **single dominant content block** of the page. - `
` is a **self-contained, independently distributable** piece of content (could be syndicated via RSS). - `
` provides an **accessible title** read by screen readers. - `

topics.title: Styling Web Page Using CSS: Introduction, Inline Styles, Embedded Style Sheets, Linking External Style Sheets, Positioning Elements, Absolute Positioning, z-index, Relative Positioning, Span, Backgrounds, Element Dimensions, Box Model and Text Flow, Media Types and Media Queries, Drop-Down Menus
